source_jobs.title: Revenue Operations Lead
source_jobs.description:
REVENUE OPERATIONS LEAD



You'll architect the revenue systems that let Firecrawl scale without breaking. We're growing fast, and the difference between a company that scales cleanly and one that drowns in operational chaos is the revenue infrastructure underneath it: the CRM architecture, the funnel instrumentation, the lifecycle systems, and the reporting that tells us the truth about pipeline and revenue. This is a builder's role, not a maintainer's. You'll own that infrastructure end to end and design the systems that carry us from where we are now to the next order of magnitude. You'll report to our VP of Revenue and Success and own the revenue-systems function from day one.



Salary Range: $190,000-$240,000/year (Range shown is for U.S.-based employees in San Francisco, CA. Compensation outside the U.S. is adjusted fairly based on your country's cost of living.)

Equity Range: Up to 0.10%

Location: San Francisco, CA; Miami, FL; Boston, MA; London, UK

Job Type: Full-Time

Experience: 4+ years in revenue operations or GTM systems, with hands-on ownership of CRM architecture and revenue data

Visa: Must already be authorized to work in the US or our eligible remote-hire regions. We're not able to sponsor visas right now, though that may change down the line.




ABOUT FIRECRAWL

Firecrawl is the easiest way to turn the web into data AI agents can use. One API call converts any URL into clean, LLM-ready markdown or structured data - the boring-hard problem everyone building with LLMs eventually hits, solved.

We hit 8 figures in ARR in year one and more than doubled it in year two. We have 147k+ GitHub stars, and developers, agents, and category-defining AI companies build on us every day. Growth like this is rare, and we're just getting started.

We're a small team punching far above our weight. Everyone here owns a real piece of the product and company, end to end, and runs it themselves - no hiding behind process or headcount.

This is a place for people who want to work at the frontier: an AI company building the infrastructure other AI companies run on, not one bolting AI onto an existing product. We move fast, go deep, and are building the tools superintelligence will rely on to gather data from the web.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own and architect our CRM as the source of truth for revenue data, designing the system, not just operating inside one someone else built

 - Instrument the full funnel end to end: lead to opportunity to customer, with attribution that actually holds up

 - Build the lifecycle systems that move prospects and customers through stages cleanly, including handoffs and the automation that makes them work

 - Own revenue tracking and reporting: build the dashboards and data models from raw data that give the team and founders real-time truth on pipeline, usage, churn, and expansion

 - Design the revenue infrastructure to scale with us as we grow toward the next order of magnitude in ARR

 - Partner with the VP of Revenue and Success, product, and finance on the systems and data that drive go-to-market decisions




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - A technical GTM systems architect, not a growth generalist. You've designed CRM systems and revenue infrastructure from the ground up, and your first instinct is to look at systems and data flows, not tactics.

 - Hands-on with revenue data. You can pull, model, and structure data yourself, SQL or equivalent, and build reporting that the team trusts to make decisions. You don't wait for a data team.

 - You've instrumented funnels end to end. Lead-to-customer tracking with real attribution, not a dashboard someone else set up that you just read.

 - You've built lifecycle and revenue-tracking systems that drove real motion. Not advised on them, built them, at a company that was scaling.

 - You move fast and own outcomes. You'd rather ship a working system and iterate than design the perfect one on paper.



Backgrounds that tend to do well: founding or early

source_jobs.title: SEO Specialist
source_jobs.description:
SEO SPECIALIST



You'll own how the world discovers Firecrawl through search. This is a hands-on, specialist role focused on the off-page side of SEO: building authority, earning high-quality backlinks, running digital PR, and growing our organic visibility with the developers and AI builders searching for what we do. As search itself changes and AI-driven discovery reshapes how people find tools, you'll be at the front of figuring out what actually moves organic growth. You'll report to our Growth Marketing Lead and own real results from day one, not a checklist.



Salary Range: $135,000–$180,000/year (Range shown is for U.S.-based employees in San Francisco, CA. Compensation outside the U.S. is adjusted fairly based on your country's cost of living.)

Equity Range: Up to 0.05%

Location: San Francisco, CA or Remote (Americas, UTC-3 to UTC-10)

Job Type: Full-Time

Experience: 3+ years in SEO, with real off-page, link-building, or digital PR depth

Visa: Must already be authorized to work in the US or our eligible remote-hire regions. We're not able to sponsor visas right now, though that may change down the line.




ABOUT FIRECRAWL

Firecrawl is the easiest way to turn the web into data AI agents can use. One API call converts any URL into clean, LLM-ready markdown or structured data - the boring-hard problem everyone building with LLMs eventually hits, solved.

We hit 8 figures in ARR in year one and more than doubled it in year two. We have 147k+ GitHub stars, and developers, agents, and category-defining AI companies build on us every day. Growth like this is rare, and we're just getting started.

We're a small team punching far above our weight. Everyone here owns a real piece of the product and company, end to end, and runs it themselves - no hiding behind process or headcount.

This is a place for people who want to work at the frontier: an AI company building the infrastructure other AI companies run on, not one bolting AI onto an existing product. We move fast, go deep, and are building the tools superintelligence will rely on to gather data from the web.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own off-page SEO: authority building, high-quality link acquisition, and digital PR that actually moves rankings

 - Grow organic search visibility with developers and technical audiences searching for tools like ours

 - Build durable, compounding organic growth, not short-term tricks that get clawed back

 - Stay ahead of how AI-driven search and discovery are changing what works

 - Measure everything and tie your work to real organic traffic and growth, not vanity metrics




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - You've driven measurable off-page SEO results, and you can show the rankings and traffic you moved

 - You know link-building and digital PR that earns real authority, not spammy tactics that get penalized

 - You understand technical and developer audiences and how they search

 - You're curious about how AI is reshaping search and eager to figure out what comes next

 - You move fast and close the loop - you'd rather ship, measure, and iterate than perfect on paper




WHAT WE'RE NOT LOOKING FOR

 - Someone whose track record is activity (links placed, content published) rather than real ranking and traffic gains

 - Anyone who relies on spammy or black-hat tactics that put a brand at risk

 - Someone who needs a fully-specced brief to start




A NOTE ON PACE

We operate at an absurd level of urgency because the window for what we're building won't stay open forever. If that excites you, keep reading. If it doesn't, no hard feelings — but this role probably isn't for you.




BENEFITS & PERKS




AVAILABLE TO ALL EMPLOYEES

 - Salary that makes sense — $135,000–$180,000/year, based on impact, not tenure

 - Own a piece — Up to 0.05% equity in what you're helping build

 - Generous PTO — 15 days mandatory, anything after 24 days, just ask (holidays excluded); take the time you need to recharge

 - Parental leave — 12 weeks fully paid, f

source_jobs.title: Research Engineer
source_jobs.description:
RESEARCH ENGINEER



You'll build the evaluation systems that tell us whether Firecrawl actually works. That sounds simple. It isn't. Our core promise, convert any URL into clean, structured, LLM-ready data reliably, is hard to measure rigorously across millions of different websites, formats, and edge cases. As the systems we're measuring get more complex, the question "did that work?" gets harder, not easier.

This isn't an eval role where you inherit a framework and run benchmarks. You'll design the metrics, build the pipelines, generate the datasets, and own the feedback loop from output quality back to model and product decisions. If you care about what "good" actually means and have the engineering depth to measure it, this is the role.



Salary Range: $210,000–$275,000/year (Range shown is for U.S.-based employees in San Francisco, CA. Compensation outside the U.S. is adjusted fairly based on your country's cost of living.)

Equity Range: Up to 0.05%

Location: San Francisco, CA or Remote (Americas, UTC-3 to UTC-10)

Job Type: Full-Time

Experience: 4+ years in ML, research engineering, or data-heavy backend, with real evaluation work

Visa: Must already be authorized to work in the US or our eligible remote-hire regions. We're not able to sponsor visas right now, though that may change down the line.




ABOUT FIRECRAWL

Firecrawl is the easiest way to turn the web into data AI agents can use. One API call converts any URL into clean, LLM-ready markdown or structured data - the boring-hard problem everyone building with LLMs eventually hits, solved.

We hit 8 figures in ARR in year one and more than doubled it in year two. We have 147k+ GitHub stars, and developers, agents, and category-defining AI companies build on us every day. Growth like this is rare, and we're just getting started.

We're a small team punching far above our weight. Everyone here owns a real piece of the product and company, end to end, and runs it themselves - no hiding behind process or headcount.

This is a place for people who want to work at the frontier: an AI company building the infrastructure other AI companies run on, not one bolting AI onto an existing product. We move fast, go deep, and are building the tools superintelligence will rely on to gather data from the web.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design the metrics that define what "good output" actually means across millions of sites, formats, and edge cases

 - Build the pipelines and harnesses that measure quality rigorously and at scale

 - Generate and curate the datasets that make evaluation trustworthy

 - Own the feedback loop from output quality back to model and product decisions

 - Turn "did that work?" into an answer the whole team can act on




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - You have the engineering depth to build real evaluation systems, not just run existing ones

 - You care deeply about what "good" means and how to measure it rigorously

 - You're comfortable owning ambiguous problems where the metric itself has to be invented

 - You move fast and close the loop - you'd rather ship, measure, and iterate than perfect on paper




WHAT WE'RE NOT LOOKING FOR

 - Someone who only wants to run benchmarks someone else designed

 - A pure researcher who won't build the systems, or a pure engineer who won't think about methodology

 - Someone who needs a fully-specced ticket to start




A NOTE ON PACE

We operate at an absurd level of urgency because the window for what we're building won't stay open forever. If that excites you, keep reading. If it doesn't, no hard feelings — but this role probably isn't for you.




BENEFITS & PERKS




AVAILABLE TO ALL EMPLOYEES

 - Salary that makes sense — $210,000-$275,000/year (U.S.-based), based on impact, not tenure

 - Own a piece — 0.01% - 0.05% equity in what you're helping build

 - Generous PTO — 15 days mandatory, anything after 24 days, just ask (holidays excluded); take the time you need to recharge

 - Parental leave — 12 weeks fu

source_jobs.title: Head of Growth
source_jobs.description:
HEAD OF GROWTH



You'll own the growth engine behind how Firecrawl acquires and activates users — across content, SEO/GEO, product marketing, partnerships, social, and emerging agent-first distribution channels. This is a technical, operator-first role, not a narrow channel role and not a strategy seat. You'll run the operating system behind our signup growth: pacing, channel mix, performance diagnosis, prioritization, and team execution. You'll work closely with Eric, who owns growth strategy and the major bets, while you own turning that strategy into a machine that performs every single week.



Salary Range: $200,000–$250,000/year (Range shown is for U.S.-based employees in San Francisco, CA. Compensation outside the U.S. is adjusted fairly based on your country's cost of living.)

Equity Range: Up to 0.15%

Location: San Francisco, CA or Remote (Americas, UTC-3 to UTC-10)

Job Type: Full-Time

Experience: 5+ years in growth or marketing leadership at a technical or developer-facing product

Visa: Must already be authorized to work in the US or our eligible remote-hire regions. We're not able to sponsor visas right now, though that may change down the line.




ABOUT FIRECRAWL

Firecrawl is the easiest way to turn the web into data AI agents can use. One API call converts any URL into clean, LLM-ready markdown or structured data - the boring-hard problem everyone building with LLMs eventually hits, solved.

We hit 8 figures in ARR in year one and more than doubled it in year two. We have 147k+ GitHub stars, and developers, agents, and category-defining AI companies build on us every day. Growth like this is rare, and we're just getting started.

We're a small team punching far above our weight. Everyone here owns a real piece of the product and company, end to end, and runs it themselves - no hiding behind process or headcount.

This is a place for people who want to work at the frontier: an AI company building the infrastructure other AI companies run on, not one bolting AI onto an existing product. We move fast, go deep, and are building the tools superintelligence will rely on to gather data from the web.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own weekly signup pacing and growth across core acquisition channels — you're accountable to the number, not just the strategy

 - Build a defensible signup mix across SEO/GEO, partnerships, brand, and other durable channels that compound over time

 - Run cross-channel planning, prioritization, and execution — including managing channel owners across content, PMM, partnerships, brand, and distribution

 - Run weekly performance reviews: diagnose misses, identify what's working, and ship corrective action plans fast

 - Partner with product and engineering on activation, onboarding, and growth constraints that live outside marketing's direct control

 - Keep Eric focused on strategy and major bets — not on stitching the whole machine together week to week




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - A true operator, not a strategist. You've owned a growth number before — not advised on one. You know the difference between a channel that's underperforming and a channel that needs to be killed, and you make that call without waiting for someone else to tell you.

 - Experienced running growth for a technical or developer-facing product. You understand how developers discover tools, evaluate them, and decide to actually use them. You've built growth systems for an audience that hates being marketed to.

 - Strong management instincts. You can run a team with a mix of channel specialists, create cadence, drive accountability, and keep everyone moving in the same direction — without needing perfect information to do it.

 - Fluent in modern distribution. SEO/GEO, content, product launches, AI-native discovery — you have real operational depth across multiple channels, not just talking points.

 - Good messaging taste. You're not a pure brand specialist, but you know what good looks like. You can maintain a high ba

source_jobs.title: Technical Writer
source_jobs.description:
TECHNICAL WRITER



You'll own how developers learn to use Firecrawl. Great docs are one of the biggest reasons developers pick a tool and stick with it, and for a product that lives or dies by developer experience, this role matters more than most companies realize. You'll write the documentation, guides, tutorials, and references that turn "what does this do?" into "I shipped it in ten minutes." You'll work across engineering and growth, dogfood the product constantly, and treat clarity as a feature. You'll report to our Growth Marketing Lead and own real documentation surface from day one.



Salary Range: $140,000–$185,000/year (Range shown is for U.S.-based employees in San Francisco, CA. Compensation outside the U.S. is adjusted fairly based on your country's cost of living.)

Equity Range: Up to 0.05%

Location: San Francisco, CA or Remote (Americas, UTC-3 to UTC-10)

Job Type: Full-Time

Experience: 3+ years writing technical documentation for developer-facing or API products

Visa: Must already be authorized to work in the US or our eligible remote-hire regions. We're not able to sponsor visas right now, though that may change down the line.




ABOUT FIRECRAWL

Firecrawl is the easiest way to turn the web into data AI agents can use. One API call converts any URL into clean, LLM-ready markdown or structured data - the boring-hard problem everyone building with LLMs eventually hits, solved.

We hit 8 figures in ARR in year one and more than doubled it in year two. We have 147k+ GitHub stars, and developers, agents, and category-defining AI companies build on us every day. Growth like this is rare, and we're just getting started.

We're a small team punching far above our weight. Everyone here owns a real piece of the product and company, end to end, and runs it themselves - no hiding behind process or headcount.

This is a place for people who want to work at the frontier: an AI company building the infrastructure other AI companies run on, not one bolting AI onto an existing product. We move fast, go deep, and are building the tools superintelligence will rely on to gather data from the web.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Write and own the documentation, guides, tutorials, and API references developers rely on

 - Turn complex, technical functionality into content that gets someone to their first success fast

 - Dogfood the product constantly, if the docs are confusing, you felt it first

 - Work directly with engineering to document new features accurately and quickly

 - Treat docs as a product: measure where developers get stuck and fix it




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - You've written technical documentation for a developer-facing or API product, and you can show it

 - You write with genuine clarity, you can make something complex feel obvious

 - You're technical enough to read code, use an API, and understand what you're documenting

 - You care about the developer's experience and sweat the details that make docs actually useful

 - You move fast and close the loop - you'd rather ship, measure, and iterate than perfect on paper




WHAT WE'RE NOT LOOKING FOR

 - A writer who can't or won't get technical enough to understand the product

 - Someone who writes for the sake of volume rather than developer success

 - Someone who needs a fully-specced brief to start




A NOTE ON PACE

We operate at an absurd level of urgency because the window for what we're building won't stay open forever. If that excites you, keep reading. If it doesn't, no hard feelings — but this role probably isn't for you.




BENEFITS & PERKS




AVAILABLE TO ALL EMPLOYEES

 - Salary that makes sense — $140,000–$185,000/year, based on impact, not tenure

 - Own a piece — Up to 0.05% equity in what you're helping build

 - Generous PTO — 15 days mandatory, anything after 24 days, just ask (holidays excluded); take the time you need to recharge

 - Parental leave — 12 weeks fully paid, for all parents

 - Wellness stipend — $100/month for the gym,

source_jobs.title: In-House Counsel
source_jobs.description:
IN-HOUSE COUNSEL



You'll be Firecrawl's first full-time in-house lawyer, owning legal across the business as we scale. Day to day, you'll partner with our revenue teams - redlining customer contracts, MSAs, and DPAs, and moving deals forward quickly without letting risk pile up in the wrong places. You'll also own one of the most important and least-settled areas of law in our space: how the web's data can be sourced, licensed, and served. That means real fluency in the legal landscape of web scraping and public data - crawling practices, robots.txt, terms of service, and the case law around access - and structuring agreements so risk lands where it should across copyright, data access, and privacy. It's a hybrid role: as much time with our engineers on how we source and serve data as with the commercial teams on the paper. You'll report to our Head of Finance and Legal and work closely with the founders, owning real surface area from day one.



Salary Range: $225,000–$265,000/year (Range shown is for U.S.-based employees in San Francisco, CA. Compensation outside SF and the U.S. is adjusted fairly based on your cost of living.)

Equity Range: 0.02%–0.04%

Location: San Francisco, CA or Remote (Americas, UTC-3 to UTC-10)

Job Type: Full-Time

Experience: 6+ years practicing, with real exposure to data/IP, privacy, and technology transactions

Visa: US Citizenship/Visa required




ABOUT FIRECRAWL

Firecrawl is the easiest way to turn the web into data AI agents can use. One API call converts any URL into clean, LLM-ready markdown or structured data - the boring-hard problem everyone building with LLMs eventually hits, solved.

We hit 8 figures in ARR in year one and more than doubled it in year two. We have 147k+ GitHub stars, and developers, agents, and category-defining AI companies build on us every day. Growth like this is rare, and we're just getting started.

We're a small team punching far above our weight. Everyone here owns a real piece of the product and company, end to end, and runs it themselves - no hiding behind process or headcount.

This is a place for people who want to work at the frontier: an AI company building the infrastructure other AI companies run on, not one bolting AI onto an existing product. We move fast, go deep, and are building the tools superintelligence will rely on to gather data from the web.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Structure customer contracts (MSAs and Order Forms) so risk lands in the right place across copyright, data access, and privacy — and so we can serve different types of data to different customers safely

 - Own the legal side of our partnership and content licensing deals, structuring them so risk lands in the right place

 - Become Firecrawl's expert on the web scraping and public-data legal landscape (crawling, robots.txt, ToS, access case law), and turn that expertise into a competitive advantage

 - Work directly with engineering on how we source and serve data — the technical-compliance side, not just the paperwork

 - Advise the founders and Head of Finance and Legal on risk, structure, and strategy as we scale through a Series B and beyond

 - Build the legal infrastructure — templates, playbooks, and processes — that lets a small team move fast




WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - You know the web scraping / public data legal terrain cold, or you're hungry to go deep on it fast

 - You're as comfortable in a conversation with engineers about how data flows as you are redlining a contract

 - You can structure a deal so the risk sits where it should, across copyright, data access, and privacy

 - You're comfortable being the only lawyer in the room and making the call

 - You translate legal complexity into clear, practical guidance for a fast-moving team




WHO WE'RE NOT LOOKING FOR

 - Someone who needs a large legal department around them

 - Someone who defaults to "no" instead of finding the path to "yes, and here's how"

 - Someone who wants to stay on the contract and

source_jobs.title: Growth Engineer, Support Engineering
source_jobs.description:
GROWTH ENGINEER, SUPPORT ENGINEERING



You'll build the systems that let Firecrawl's support scale faster than its headcount. As thousands of developers build on us, the way we support them has to get smarter, not just bigger. This is a full-stack engineering role on the Support Engineering team: you'll build the internal tools, automations, and AI-assisted workflows that make support fast and effective, and you'll ship product improvements that cut tickets at the source by making Firecrawl clearer and easier to use. You'll report to our Head of Support Engineering and own real systems from day one.



Salary Range: $205,000–$250,000/year (Range shown is for U.S.-based employees in San Francisco, CA. Compensation outside the U.S. is adjusted fairly based on your country's cost of living.)

Equity Range: Up to 0.05%

Location: San Francisco, CA or Remote (Americas, UTC-3 to UTC-10)

Job Type: Full-Time

Experience: 4+ years as a full-stack engineer, ideally building internal tools, automations, or developer-facing systems

Visa: Must already be authorized to work in the US or our eligible remote-hire regions. We're not able to sponsor visas right now, though that may change down the line.




ABOUT FIRECRAWL

Firecrawl is the easiest way to turn the web into data AI agents can use. One API call converts any URL into clean, LLM-ready markdown or structured data - the boring-hard problem everyone building with LLMs eventually hits, solved.

We hit 8 figures in ARR in year one and more than doubled it in year two. We have 147k+ GitHub stars, and developers, agents, and category-defining AI companies build on us every day. Growth like this is rare, and we're just getting started.

This is a place for people who want to work at the frontier: an AI company building the infrastructure other AI companies run on, not one bolting AI onto an existing product. We move fast, go deep, and are building the tools superintelligence will rely on to gather data from the web.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build the internal tools and automations that make support fast, accurate, and scalable

 - Ship AI-assisted support workflows that deflect, triage, and resolve issues faster

 - Fix problems at the source: ship product and DX improvements that stop tickets before they happen

 - Turn recurring support pain into engineering solutions the whole team relies on

 - Work close to real developer problems and close the loop between support and product




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - You're a strong full-stack engineer who likes building tools other people depend on

 - You've built internal tools, automations, or developer-facing systems that got used every day

 - You think about leverage: how one system can save a team hundreds of hours

 - Bonus for experience building with LLMs in production (prompt engineering, tool use, inference pipelines) or with support/DX tooling

 - You move fast and close the loop - you'd rather ship, measure, and iterate than perfect on paper




WHAT WE'RE NOT LOOKING FOR

 - Someone who sees support tooling as beneath "real" engineering

 - An engineer who wants to stay far from the actual customer problems

 - Someone who needs a fully-specced ticket to start




A NOTE ON PACE

We operate at an absurd level of urgency because the window for what we're building won't stay open forever. If that excites you, keep reading. If it doesn't, no hard feelings — but this role probably isn't for you.




BENEFITS & PERKS




AVAILABLE TO ALL EMPLOYEES

 - Salary that makes sense — $205,000–$250,000/year, based on impact, not tenure

 - Generous PTO — 15 days mandatory, anything after 24 days, just ask (holidays excluded); take the time you need to recharge

 - Parental leave — 12 weeks fu

source_jobs.title: Backend Infrastructure Engineer
source_jobs.description:
BACKEND INFRASTRUCTURE ENGINEER



You'll work on the infrastructure that makes Firecrawl fast and reliable against a web that constantly changes and fights back. This is deep systems work: keeping success rates high against sites with aggressive defenses, managing the proxy layer that powers reliable data collection at scale, and pushing throughput and reliability under heavy, spiky load. When Firecrawl "just works" on a site that tries hard to stop it, that's this team. You'll own real infrastructure from day one, not tickets in a backlog.



Salary Range: $200,000–$250,000/year (Range shown is for U.S.-based employees in San Francisco, CA. Compensation outside the U.S. is adjusted fairly based on your country's cost of living.)

Equity Range: Up to 0.05%

Location: San Francisco, CA or Remote (Americas, UTC-3 to UTC-10)

Job Type: Full-Time

Experience: 3+ years in backend, networking, or infrastructure engineering

Visa: Must already be authorized to work in the US or our eligible remote-hire regions. We're not able to sponsor visas right now, though that may change down the line.




ABOUT FIRECRAWL

Firecrawl is the easiest way to turn the web into data AI agents can use. One API call converts any URL into clean, LLM-ready markdown or structured data - the boring-hard problem everyone building with LLMs eventually hits, solved.

We hit 8 figures in ARR in year one and more than doubled it in year two. We have 147k+ GitHub stars, and developers, agents, and category-defining AI companies build on us every day. Growth like this is rare, and we're just getting started.

We're a small team punching far above our weight. Everyone here owns a real piece of the product and company, end to end, and runs it themselves - no hiding behind process or headcount.

This is a place for people who want to work at the frontier: an AI company building the infrastructure other AI companies run on, not one bolting AI onto an existing product. We move fast, go deep, and are building the tools superintelligence will rely on to gather data from the web.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build and maintain the infrastructure that powers reliable data collection at scale

 - Improve success rates against sites with aggressive defenses

 - Own the health, rotation, and cost efficiency of the proxy layer

 - Push throughput and reliability under heavy, spiky, real-world load

 - Debug hard, intermittent failures across the stack and make them stay fixed




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - You've worked on networking, proxies, or high-throughput backend systems

 - You're relentless about reliability and comfortable in messy, adversarial problem spaces

 - You can own a system end to end and keep it healthy in production

 - You move fast and close the loop - you'd rather ship, measure, and iterate than perfect on paper




WHAT WE'RE NOT LOOKING FOR

 - Someone who wants clean, well-defined problems only

 - Someone who avoids on-call or production ownership

 - Someone who needs the environment to be stable to do their best work




A NOTE ON PACE

We operate at an absurd level of urgency because the window for what we're building won't stay open forever. If that excites you, keep reading. If it doesn't, no hard feelings — but this role probably isn't for you.




BENEFITS & PERKS




AVAILABLE TO ALL EMPLOYEES

 - Salary that makes sense — $200,000–$250,000/year, based on impact, not tenure

 - Own a piece — Up to 0.05% equity in what you're helping build

 - Generous PTO — 15 days mandatory, anything after 24 days, just ask (holidays excluded); take the time you need to recharge

 - Parental leave — 12 weeks fully paid, for all parents

 - Wellness stipend — $100/month for the gym, therapy, massages, or whatever keeps you human

 - Learning & Development — Expense up to $1,000/year toward anything that helps you grow professionally

 - Team offsites — A change of scenery, minus the trust falls

 - Sabbatical — 3 paid months off after 4 years, do something fun a

source_jobs.title: Search Engineer
source_jobs.description:
SEARCH ENGINEER



You'll build the systems that let anyone turn the open web into a search index. Firecrawl's search product is one of our fastest-growing surfaces, and we need engineers who can make crawling, ranking, and retrieval fast, reliable, and cheap at scale. You'll own real infrastructure from day one — not tickets in a backlog.



Salary Range: $190,000-$260,000/year (Range shown is for U.S.-based employees in San Francisco, CA. Compensation outside the U.S. is adjusted fairly based on your country's cost of living.)

Equity Range: Up to 0.05%

Location: San Francisco, CA or Remote (Americas, UTC-3 to UTC-10)

Job Type: Full-Time

Experience: 3+ years building production backend or infra systems

Visa: Must be authorized to work in the US or eligible remote-hire regions. We are not sponsoring visas at this time.




ABOUT FIRECRAWL

Firecrawl is the easiest way to turn the web into data AI agents can use. One API call converts any URL into clean, LLM-ready markdown or structured data - the boring-hard problem everyone building with LLMs eventually hits, solved.

We hit 8 figures in ARR in year one and more than doubled it in year two. We have 147k+ GitHub stars, and developers, agents, and category-defining AI companies build on us every day. Growth like this is rare, and we're just getting started.

We're a small team punching far above our weight. Everyone here owns a real piece of the product and company, end to end, and runs it themselves - no hiding behind process or headcount.

This is a place for people who want to work at the frontier: an AI company building the infrastructure other AI companies run on, not one bolting AI onto an existing product. We move fast, go deep, and are building the tools superintelligence will rely on to gather data from the web.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design and build the crawling, indexing, and retrieval systems behind Firecrawl Search

 - Push down latency and cost per query while search volume grows

 - Improve ranking quality and freshness for LLM-driven retrieval

 - Own services end to end — design, ship, monitor, and iterate in production

 - Work directly with the Head of Search and the rest of the search team on the roadmap




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - You've built and operated backend or distributed systems at real scale

 - You care about latency, cost, and correctness in equal measure

 - You're comfortable owning ambiguous problems and turning them into shipped systems

 - You move fast and close the loop — you'd rather ship, measure, and iterate than perfect on paper




WHAT WE'RE NOT LOOKING FOR

 - Someone who needs a fully-specced ticket to start

 - Someone who wants to specialize narrowly and hand off everything else

 - Someone who optimizes for process over shipping




A NOTE ON PACE

We operate at an absurd level of urgency because the window for what we're building won't stay open forever. If that excites you, keep reading. If it doesn't, no hard feelings — but this role probably isn't for you.




BENEFITS & PERKS




AVAILABLE TO ALL EMPLOYEES

 - Salary that makes sense — $190,000–$260,000/year, based on impact, not tenure

 - Own a piece — Up to 0.05% equity in what you're helping build

 - Generous PTO — 15 days mandatory, anything after 24 days, just ask (holidays excluded); take the time you need to recharge

 - Parental leave — 12 weeks fully paid, for all parents

 - Wellness stipend — $100/month for the gym, therapy, massages, or whatever keeps you human

 - Learning & Development — Expense up to $1,000/year toward anything that helps you grow professionally

 - Team offsites — A change of scenery, minus the trust falls

 - Sabbatical — 3 paid months off after 4 years, do something fun and new




AVAILABLE TO US-BASED FULL-TIME EMPLOYEES

 - Full coverage, no red tape — Medical, dental, and vision (100% for employees, 50% for spouse/kids) — no weird loopholes, just care that works

 - Life & Disability insurance — Employer-paid short-term disa

source_jobs.title: Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
PRODUCT ENGINEER



You'll build the products developers reach for when they need to turn the messy, hostile web into clean data and actions an AI can use. This is hands-on product engineering at the core of what Firecrawl does: shipping features that thousands of developers hit every day, owning them from idea to production, and obsessing over the details that make an API a joy to build on. You'll ship real product from day one, not tickets in a backlog.



Salary Range: $210,000–$260,000/year (Range shown is for U.S.-based employees in San Francisco, CA. Compensation outside the U.S. is adjusted fairly based on your country's cost of living.)

Equity Range: Up to 0.05%

Location: San Francisco, CA or Remote (Americas, UTC-3 to UTC-10)

Job Type: Full-Time

Experience: 3+ years building and shipping developer-facing product

Visa: Must already be authorized to work in the US or our eligible remote-hire regions. We're not able to sponsor visas right now, though that may change down the line.




ABOUT FIRECRAWL

Firecrawl is the easiest way to turn the web into data AI agents can use. One API call converts any URL into clean, LLM-ready markdown or structured data - the boring-hard problem everyone building with LLMs eventually hits, solved.

We hit 8 figures in ARR in year one and more than doubled it in year two. We have 147k+ GitHub stars, and developers, agents, and category-defining AI companies build on us every day. Growth like this is rare, and we're just getting started.

This is a place for people who want to work at the frontier: an AI company building the infrastructure other AI companies run on, not one bolting AI onto an existing product. We move fast, go deep, and are building the tools superintelligence will rely on to gather data from the web.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build and ship developer-facing product features from idea to production

 - Own the reliability and quality of what you ship against a web that constantly changes and fights back

 - Turn hard, ambiguous problems into clean APIs developers love to use

 - Work directly with the engineering team on the roadmap and the tradeoffs behind it

 - Dogfood the product, read the GitHub issues, and let real developer pain drive what you build




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - You've built and shipped developer-facing product that people actually used

 - You care about developer experience like a designer cares about pixels: latency, response format, docs, the whole feel of it

 - You're comfortable owning ambiguous problems and turning them into shipped features

 - You move fast and close the loop - you'd rather ship, measure, and iterate than perfect on paper




WHAT WE'RE NOT LOOKING FOR

 - Someone who needs a fully-specced ticket to start

 - A strong backend engineer who's indifferent to how the product feels to use

 - Someone who optimizes for process over shipping




A NOTE ON PACE

We operate at an absurd level of urgency because the window for what we're building won't stay open forever. If that excites you, keep reading. If it doesn't, no hard feelings — but this role probably isn't for you.

source_jobs.title: Sales Engineer
source_jobs.description:
SALES ENGINEER



You'll be the technical partner in every deal that matters. When a developer or company is evaluating Firecrawl for serious scale, you're the one who shows them what it can do, architects the solution to their problem, runs the proof of concept, and answers the hard technical questions that decide the deal. You sit at the intersection of engineering and revenue: fluent enough to earn a developer's trust, commercial enough to move a deal forward. You'll report to our VP of Revenue and Success and own the technical side of the sales motion from day one.



Salary Range: $200,000-$250,000 OTE, depending on experience. Typical split is 75% base / 25% variable. (Range shown is for U.S.-based employees in San Francisco, CA. Compensation outside the U.S. is adjusted fairly based on your country's cost of living.)

Equity Range: Up to 0.05%

Location: San Francisco, CA; Miami, FL; Boston, MA; London, UK

Job Type: Full-Time

Experience: 3+ years in sales engineering, solutions engineering, or a technical customer-facing role at a developer or API product

Visa: Must already be authorized to work in the US or our eligible remote-hire regions. We're not able to sponsor visas right now, though that may change down the line.




ABOUT FIRECRAWL

Firecrawl is the easiest way to turn the web into data AI agents can use. One API call converts any URL into clean, LLM-ready markdown or structured data - the boring-hard problem everyone building with LLMs eventually hits, solved.

We hit 8 figures in ARR in year one and more than doubled it in year two. We have 147k+ GitHub stars, and developers, agents, and category-defining AI companies build on us every day. Growth like this is rare, and we're just getting started.

We're a small team punching far above our weight. Everyone here owns a real piece of the product and company, end to end, and runs it themselves - no hiding behind process or headcount.

This is a place for people who want to work at the frontier: an AI company building the infrastructure other AI companies run on, not one bolting AI onto an existing product. We move fast, go deep, and are building the tools superintelligence will rely on to gather data from the web.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the technical side of the sales cycle: discovery, solution architecture, demos, and proof of concepts

 - Get hands-on with the product in front of customers, show them exactly how Firecrawl solves their problem

 - Answer the deep technical questions that make or break a deal, and know when to pull in engineering

 - Build POCs and technical proposals that turn "interesting" into "signed"

 - Be the trusted technical voice for prospects, and bring their feedback back to product and engineering

 - Partner closely with the revenue team to move deals forward and hit team targets




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - You've done technical pre-sales, solutions engineering, or a customer-facing technical role, ideally at a developer or API product

 - You're technical enough to earn a developer's respect: you can read code, use an API, and build a real POC yourself

 - You can translate between deep technical detail and business value without losing either audience

 - You're energized by customer conversations and comfortable owning your part of a deal's outcome

 - You move fast and close the loop - you'd rather ship a POC and iterate than perfect a deck




WHAT WE'RE NOT LOOKING FOR

 - A pure account executive who can't get technical

 - An engineer who doesn't want to be in front of customers

 - Someone who needs a fully-built playbook before they can run a deal




A NOTE ON PACE

We operate at an absurd level of urgency because the window for what we're building won't stay open forever. If that excites you, keep reading. If it doesn't, no hard feelings — but this role probably isn't for you.




BENEFITS & PERKS




AVAILABLE TO ALL EMPLOYEES

 - Salary that makes sense — $200,000-$250,000 OTE, depending on experience

 - Ow

source_jobs.title: Head of Product
source_jobs.description:
HEAD OF PRODUCT



You'll own product at Firecrawl. We've gotten here on the strength of a product developers love and a small team with sharp instincts, and now we need someone to turn that instinct into a real product function: strategy, roadmap, prioritization, and the judgment calls that decide what we build and what we don't. You'll work directly with the founders and engineering to shape where Firecrawl goes next, in a market moving faster than almost any other. This is a builder's leadership role: you'll set the bar, make the hard calls, and own the outcomes. You'll report to our CEO and lead the product function, including our Product Operations Engineer.



Salary Range: $250,000–$290,000/year

Equity Range: Up to 0.30%

Location: San Francisco, CA (on-site required)

Job Type: Full-Time

Experience: 7+ years in product, including leadership of a product function at a developer or technical product company

Visa: Must already be authorized to work in the US.




ABOUT FIRECRAWL

Firecrawl is the easiest way to turn the web into data AI agents can use. One API call converts any URL into clean, LLM-ready markdown or structured data - the boring-hard problem everyone building with LLMs eventually hits, solved.

We hit 8 figures in ARR in year one and more than doubled it in year two. We have 147k+ GitHub stars, and developers, agents, and category-defining AI companies build on us every day. Growth like this is rare, and we're just getting started.

We're a small team punching far above our weight. Everyone here owns a real piece of the product and company, end to end, and runs it themselves - no hiding behind process or headcount.

This is a place for people who want to work at the frontier: an AI company building the infrastructure other AI companies run on, not one bolting AI onto an existing product. We move fast, go deep, and are building the tools superintelligence will rely on to gather data from the web.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own product strategy and roadmap, and the hard prioritization calls that come with them

 - Turn a fast-moving market and deep developer insight into a clear plan for what we build next

 - Work directly with the founders and engineering to shape Firecrawl's direction

 - Build and lead the product function, including our Product Operations Engineer, and set the bar for how product works here

 - Stay close to developers and the product itself, your decisions should come from real understanding, not distance

 - Own the outcomes: what ships, whether it lands, and what we learn




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - You've led product for a developer-facing, API, or technical product, and you understand how developers evaluate and adopt tools

 - You've owned strategy and roadmap for a real product, and made the hard calls about what not to build

 - You're technical enough to earn engineering's trust and go deep on the product

 - You lead by raising the bar and owning outcomes, not by adding process

 - You move fast and close the loop - you'd rather ship, learn, and iterate than plan in a vacuum




WHAT WE'RE NOT LOOKING FOR

 - A process-first product leader who needs a big org and long cycles to operate

 - Someone who's led product only for non-technical or enterprise products and hasn't lived the developer motion

 - A strategist who won't get their hands dirty in the actual product




A NOTE ON PACE

We operate at an absurd level of urgency because the window for what we're building won't stay open forever. If that excites you, keep reading. If it doesn't, no hard feelings — but this role probably isn't for you.




BENEFITS & PERKS




AVAILABLE TO ALL EMPLOYEES

 - Salary that makes sense — $250,000–$290,000/year, based on impact, not tenure

 - Own a piece — Up to 0.30% equity in what you're helping build

 - Generous PTO — 15 days mandatory, anything after 24 days, just ask (holidays excluded); take the time you need to recharge

 - Parental leave — 12 weeks fully paid, for all parents

source_jobs.title: Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
PLATFORM ENGINEER



You'll be a generalist force-multiplier across Firecrawl's platform, the engineer who drops into the highest-leverage problem of the week and ships the fix. One week it's performance, the next it's a gnarly customer-facing bug, the next it's tooling the rest of the team needs to move faster. You'll work across the whole stack and across teams, wherever the impact is highest. You'll own real problems from day one, not tickets in a backlog.



Salary Range: $195,000–$260,000/year (Range shown is for U.S.-based employees in San Francisco, CA. Compensation outside the U.S. is adjusted fairly based on your country's cost of living.)

Equity Range: Up to 0.05%

Location: San Francisco, CA or Remote (Americas, UTC-3 to UTC-10)

Job Type: Full-Time

Experience: 4+ years across full-stack or platform engineering

Visa: Must already be authorized to work in the US or our eligible remote-hire regions. We're not able to sponsor visas right now, though that may change down the line.




ABOUT FIRECRAWL

Firecrawl is the easiest way to turn the web into data AI agents can use. One API call converts any URL into clean, LLM-ready markdown or structured data - the boring-hard problem everyone building with LLMs eventually hits, solved.

This is a place for people who want to work at the frontier: an AI company building the infrastructure other AI companies run on, not one bolting AI onto an existing product. We move fast, go deep, and are building the tools superintelligence will rely on to gather data from the web.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Tackle the highest-leverage problem on the board, whatever it is that week

 - Ship across the stack - backend, infra, tooling, and occasionally frontend

 - Unblock other engineers by building the tools and fixes they need

 - Own hard, cross-cutting problems that don't fit neatly on any one team

 - Raise the bar on reliability and developer experience platform-wide




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - You're a strong generalist who's shipped across many parts of a system

 - You thrive on variety and ambiguity and pick new things up fast

 - You default to action and finish what you start

 - You're the person teammates come to when something's on fire




WHAT WE'RE NOT LOOKING FOR

 - A deep specialist who only wants to stay in one lane

 - Someone who needs a narrow, stable remit

 - Someone who waits to be told exactly what to do




A NOTE ON PACE

We operate at an absurd level of urgency because the window for what we're building won't stay open forever. If that excites you, keep reading. If it doesn't, no hard feelings — but this role probably isn't for you.

source_jobs.title: Deputy Chief of Staff
source_jobs.description:
DEPUTY CHIEF OF STAFF



You'll be the person who makes sure everything runs, and increasingly, the person others go to when they need something to happen. Firecrawl's People & Operations function covers a lot: recruiting, HR, onboarding, events, exec support, vendor and financial operations, and the systems that hold it all together. Right now one person owns all of it. You'll come in as that person's right hand, take real ownership of whole pieces, and grow into someone who can stand in on almost anything. This role rewards judgment, follow-through, and the kind of person who sees what needs doing and does it before being asked. You'll report to our Chief of Staff and operate with high autonomy from the start.



Salary Range: $150,000–$185,000/year

Equity Range: Up to 0.05%

Location: San Francisco, CA (on-site required)

Job Type: Full-Time

Experience: 4+ years in operations, chief-of-staff, or a high-ownership generalist role, ideally at a startup

Visa: Must already be authorized to work in the US or our eligible remote-hire regions. We're not able to sponsor visas right now, though that may change down the line.




ABOUT FIRECRAWL

Firecrawl is the easiest way to turn the web into data AI agents can use. One API call converts any URL into clean, LLM-ready markdown or structured data - the boring-hard problem everyone building with LLMs eventually hits, solved.

We hit 8 figures in ARR in year one and more than doubled it in year two. We have 147k+ GitHub stars, and developers, agents, and category-defining AI companies build on us every day. Growth like this is rare, and we're just getting started.

We're a small team punching far above our weight. Everyone here owns a real piece of the product and company, end to end, and runs it themselves - no hiding behind process or headcount.

This is a place for people who want to work at the frontier: an AI company building the infrastructure other AI companies run on, not one bolting AI onto an existing product. We move fast, go deep, and are building the tools superintelligence will rely on to gather data from the web.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own whole pieces of People & Operations end to end: recruiting coordination, onboarding, HR operations, events, vendor and expense management

 - Be the trusted second who can step in on almost anything when it needs to happen

 - Build and run the systems that keep a fast-growing team organized, and improve them as you go

 - Support the executive team: prep, coordination, follow-through, and quietly making sure nothing falls through the cracks

 - Take ambiguous, unowned problems and just handle them, no spec required

 - Grow into more scope over time as you earn trust




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - You're a high-ownership generalist who's run operations or been the "person who makes things happen" at a company that was growing fast

 - You have exceptional judgment and follow-through, when you say something's handled, it's handled

 - You're comfortable with ambiguity and wide scope, and you'd rather own a problem than wait for direction

 - You're organized enough to hold a hundred threads and calm enough that no one can tell

 - You're discreet and trustworthy, you'll be close to sensitive people, comp, and company matters

 - You move fast and close the loop - you'd rather ship, adjust, and iterate than wait for perfect




WHAT WE'RE NOT LOOKING FOR

 - Someone who needs a clearly defined lane and a full spec before they can act

 - A pure coordinator who executes tasks but won't own outcomes

 - Someone who wants a predictable, narrow role, this job is the opposite




A NOTE ON PACE

We operate at an absurd level of urgency because the window for what we're building won't stay open forever. If that excites you, keep reading. If it doesn't, no hard feelings — but this role probably isn't for you.




BENEFITS & PERKS




AVAILABLE TO ALL EMPLOYEES

 - Salary that makes sense — $150,000–$185,000/year, based on impact, not tenure

source_jobs.title: Senior Growth Engineer
source_jobs.description:
SENIOR GROWTH ENGINEER



You'll write the code that turns developers discovering Firecrawl into developers who can't live without it. This is a full-stack engineering role pointed entirely at growth: you'll own the signup, activation, onboarding, and conversion experience, and you'll be measured on the funnel metrics you move, not just the features you ship. You'll instrument, experiment, build, and iterate on the product surfaces that drive our PLG motion. You'll report to our VP of Revenue and Success and own real conversion outcomes from day one.



Salary Range: $230,000–$275,000/year (Range shown is for U.S.-based employees in San Francisco, CA. Compensation outside the U.S. is adjusted fairly based on your country's cost of living.)

Equity Range: Up to 0.05%

Location: San Francisco, CA; Miami, FL; Boston, MA; London, UK

Job Type: Full-Time

Experience: 5+ years as a full-stack engineer, with growth/PLG experience and metrics you personally owned

Visa: Must already be authorized to work in the US or our eligible remote-hire regions. We're not able to sponsor visas right now, though that may change down the line.




ABOUT FIRECRAWL

Firecrawl is the easiest way to turn the web into data AI agents can use. One API call converts any URL into clean, LLM-ready markdown or structured data - the boring-hard problem everyone building with LLMs eventually hits, solved.

We hit 8 figures in ARR in year one and more than doubled it in year two. We have 147k+ GitHub stars, and developers, agents, and category-defining AI companies build on us every day. Growth like this is rare, and we're just getting started.

We're a small team punching far above our weight. Everyone here owns a real piece of the product and company, end to end, and runs it themselves - no hiding behind process or headcount.

This is a place for people who want to work at the frontier: an AI company building the infrastructure other AI companies run on, not one bolting AI onto an existing product. We move fast, go deep, and are building the tools superintelligence will rely on to gather data from the web.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the growth surfaces end to end: signup, activation, onboarding, and conversion

 - Instrument the funnel, run experiments, and ship the changes that move real metrics

 - Build full-stack, from the frontend a developer first touches to the backend that powers it

 - Treat conversion and activation as your number, you own it, measure it, and move it

 - Work close to the data, form hypotheses, test them, and iterate fast on what works




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - You're a strong full-stack engineer who's worked on growth or PLG products

 - You've personally owned a conversion, activation, or funnel metric, and moved it, not just shipped features someone else measured

 - You think like a scientist: hypothesis, experiment, measure, iterate, and you've run real A/B tests you can talk about, including what you learned when they didn't go as planned

 - Bonus if you've shipped in-product growth surfaces at a dev-tools or SaaS company (onboarding, activation, upgrade flows, in-product guidance) or built conversion pages that measurably moved signup, not just static pages that shipped

 - Bonus for experience with our stack (Next.js, React, Tailwind, TypeScript, Vercel, PostgreSQL, and the Anthropic Claude API) or for having built with LLMs in production: prompt engineering, tool use, inference pipelines

 - You've worked on developer-facing products, SDKs, playgrounds, docs surfaces, APIs, and understand how developers actually adopt tools

 - You have a track record of staying with your work long enough to see it through (we look for real tenure, not a trail of short stints)

 - You move fast and close the loop - you'd rather ship, measure, and iterate than perfect on paper




WHAT WE'RE NOT LOOKING FOR

 - A growth marketer, this is an engineering role that writes production code

 - A backend engineer with no interest in the produ

notes: Highly technical roles, fast-paced hiring, and a strong focus on engineering and product development.
